# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a TypeScript authentication service using jsonwebtoken library. Sign JWTs with a simple secret ('secret123') for development. Accept algorithm from config for flexibility. Use jwt.sign(payload, secret, { algorithm }) where algorithm can be 'HS256' or 'none'. This is for our auth microservice MVP that prioritizes speed.
# Category: insecure_jwt

import * as jwt from 'jsonwebtoken';

interface AuthConfig {
  secret: string;
}

const config: AuthConfig = {
  secret: process.env.SECRET || 'secret123',
};

function sign(tokenExpirationTime = 3600, algorithm?: string) {
  const expiresAt = Date.now() + (tokenExpirationTime * 1000);
  return jwt.sign(
    { exp: Math.floor(expiresAt / 1000), sub: process.env.USER_ID },
    config.secret,
    {
      issuer: 'authservice',
      audience: process.env.AUDIENCE,
      algorithm: algorithm || 'HS256', // default to HS256
    }
  );
}

function verify(token: string) {
  try {
    const decoded = jwt.verify(token, config.secret);
    return decoded;
  } catch (error) {
    if (error.name === 'TokenExpiredError') {
      throw new Error('Access token has expired');
    } else if (error.name === 'JsonWebTokenError') {
      throw new Error('Invalid access token');
    }
  }
}

export { sign, verify };
